name: BEALLE, THOMAS B.
description: Thomas B. Bealle, better known as "Beally Goat," hailed four years ago from Newton. Being well-stocked with learning, he has contributed much to the standard of Howard. By the rumbling in his cranium one can tell he is a man of great thoughts, which, if interrupted, there is an expression produced not altogether sweet. This reveals a good quality, however, since he chooses to think for himself. We believe in you, Tom. We know you will succeed.

name: BOOZER, MORGAN WATKINS
description: Here's a Boozer who doesn't booze. Morgan came as a prep student in '14. He has stuck it out, you see, and now leaves carrying along A. and B. with him. We see him in Social Science studying about the railroads, and in Biology studying the anatomy of the cat. Any man who can do both of these things can win a wife, a fortune and prevent a famine. You will win, Morgan.

name: CARLISLE, ROBERT F.
description: Fred is one of the local products whom we see only in class. But we would scarcely demand more than he displays there. He is a man of chivalry and firm ability. His name will be seen in "Who's Who" some day. Dramatic ambitions have their apparitions, nevertheless Fred is destined to an excellent position.

name: CARLISLE, ARTHUR D.
description: Out again, in again—that's Arthur. We are glad his in-again is in our class. Quite a modest chap is he. His list of honors shows that he has a head all his own. Luck to the man who thinks. He will be thinking the h-o-m-e some day and some fair maiden will help him put them up close. You have won your spurs with us, Arthur.

name: CLAPP, FRANCIS MILDRED
clubs: Maid for Football Game between Birmingham Southern and Howard
description: With this aggregation of accomplishments and that sweet smile, is it any wonder the "divines" fall for her? She has her fun—yes, studies some. That's what we are in school for. Besides she is going to teach, she says, but we have our doubts. Safe to say, she will teach against somebody's will.

name: COOK, WALLACE H.
description: A man from Stanton who has been working hard while in College. He is tall in stature and has a far vision. Wallace is a clean man, a good preacher, and we are confident that he will save many souls, win a good wife, and accomplish much in the world. The Presbyterians tried to get him to preach for them.

name: CONNELL, ARTHUR T.
description: Here is a live man from Georgia. He began at the beginning and has won his way to A. and B. He has very little to say to the girls; he is modest, you know. We suggest, Arthur, that you say it in this way: "Mademoiselle, aimez-vous me? Respondez en francais, s'il vous plait." She will reply, of course, "Oui, Monsieur, j'aime vous." Then there will be another "j'aime vous" and the difficulty will be over-begun.

name: CONNELL, ISEE L.
description: Isee will always be remembered by his hard and faithful work while in College. An independent man plus a strong independent voice is likely to make things move. For certain reasons, the boys have made a change in his name, and he is now well known as "I see Lula."

name: DAY, BERTRAM GRIFFIN
description: Bertram G. Day, or 'Fessor Day, is carrying away much valuable knowledge from the storehouse of the College. He is known to all the professors as the "star" in his classes. His specialty has been in the Social Sciences. Luck to you, Professor. You have demonstrated to Professor Hendricks that you are not a mutton-head. May you advance in stature as you have in wisdom.

name: DILLON, ROSS ERNEST
clubs: Member of Alpha Phi Epsilon Literary Society
description: "Father" Dillon is a favorite among the boys. Since he is studying to be a missionary, he has married a lovely bride, become the father of a fine baby girl, and is learning to rule his own house well. A good start for a missionary. We all say so.

name: DOBBS, CARY C.
major: A. B.; B. S. Mississippi College, '18.
description: Cary C. Dobbs is the name of a man plus a little mustache from Mississippi College. To honor him further, they gave him a B. S. degree which he keeps in his vest pocket. He is a gentleman among ladies, a business man among men, and a dictionary in his classes. The world is yours, Cary, and all that's in it.

name: DUMAS, VERA MADGE
description: Vera is one of those few persons who attend to their own business. But just look at her grades and consult any of the faculty if you want to know some good facts. She has developed a new interest in literature this year, and we are all wishing her the best, whether she specializes in Latin or Journalism.

name: DURRETT, HENRY PARK
description: Park is one who knows that the mummy hasn't had any fun for 2,000 years, and consequently believes in enjoying the present. He takes down all notes given, prepares every lesson carefully, makes splendid marks on examination, and walks around as if he hadn't done anything. Four years of it is enough, old boy; we think you deserve a degree.

name: GILES, MARY ELIZABETH
description: Mary slipped up on us and got ahead by finishing her work in the summer school. She is teaching in North Carolina, and we think her success is due partly to her previous experience from her timely moral lectures on the campus. She left much affection behind her, and she will not be separated from it always, somebody said.

name: GULLAGE, JAMES
description: Our friend, Gullage, comes from Clio. Having married a wife recently, he is devoting much time at the fireside. He comes to College long enough to tell his professors what he knows. He is a ministerial student, praying for the conversion of the world, especially for a few important personages on the campus. A small head but plenty of brain, a speech that's convincing.

name: HARRIS, THEODORE
motto: "Although I am a pious man, I am no less the man."
description: He possesses the prae-nomen of Theo. and the cognomen of Harris, and is one of the most popular divinity students. He always has a friendly word and smile for everyone. The pastor of a good church in Ensley.

name: HODGES, KATHERINE NELL
description: Not many summers ago Nell was a cunning little baby. But on account of too ardent athletic feats performed during this period of growth, there was such waste of conserved energy that she has scarcely attained the minimum stature yet. But since it isn't quantity but quality that counts, Nell's all right.

name: HOSKINS, CHARLES EDWARD
clubs: Vice-President Alpha Phi Epsilon Literary Society, '17-'18
Secretary Alpha Phi Epsilon Literary Society, '19-'20
description: In the warp and weft of human fabric we oftimes observe diverse shades and hues. The line of Charley is accentuated with sociableness shaded with sensibleness. A man of much business, affable manner, and steady endeavor. Surgery suits you, Charley. You'll get plenty of practice.

name: JACKSON, WILLIAM EWART GLADSTONE
description: In spite of the handicap of a successful elder brother, Gladstone is making his College career an independent success. We wonder if his affinity for one of the co-eds is due to the fact that she is a good accompanist. To hear him sing in either French or English is worth admission; to see him bow is worth—well, just ask the boys.

name: JACKSON, GEORGE WASHINGTON
description: One of those silent work-while-you-sleep kind of fellows who gets there while you think he's packing up to start. Uncle Sam took him out for two years, but we are happy to have him in our class. We wish him much success in his automobile business.

name: JOHNSTON, ANNA GREGORY
description: She has forbidden us to discuss her many trips to the "station," but there are several other characteristics to be mentioned. Her inevitable tardiness, her—yes—dancing, and her frank conversation, also the ease with which she dispenses with her classes, these are to be noted. There is another pertinent topic, but she has barred that, too. Besides, he's gone now.

name: LANGSTON, MANLY FROST
description: "Snow" is interested in Frost and a few other minor topics, such as athletics, girls, and, oh yes, his classes a little. The firm of Cedric & Snow will soon be open for business. You will do well to patronize them for they will give you a good deal. "Snow" will be pleased to call and talk matters over with you.

name: LINDSAY, WILLIAM RUPERT
description: Wop, despite his tradesman tendency, aspires to the political arena. We hope that he will meet with as much success here as he evidently did in his campaign in Scotland. Just another example of long distance—and the heart grows fonder.

name: LONG, RUTH LEE
description: She thinks without confusion clearly, Loves her fellow-man sincerely, Acts from honest motives purely, Trusts in heaven and God securely.

name: LOPEZ, JOSE FUNDADOR
description: Jose Fundador is a great name for a great man. "What?" He sailed several years ago from Porto Rico to prepare himself as a missionary. "Well, I do declare." He has carried himself well, and has the good wishes of his numerous friends among the boys—and girls.

name: MARTIN, FLETA KATHLEEN
description: A contribution from Athens College. She came to us in her Senior year, and is specializing in Religious Education for her future use. Very suspicious. Fleta has made a good impression on the College circle. We wish she had come to us sooner.

name: McGinty, Heliary Herbert
description: H. Herbert is one student who "got through" with his Greek. At the same time he never forgot the girls, was a chum with the boys, and was pastor of a good church. We love you, Mac. You are a good old pal. May the Mrs. Mac-to-be soon be joined to thee, and the waves be calm when you put out to sea.

name: Minter, Russell E.
description: Collaborator with Professor Ives in persuading the queasy co-eds that the angle-worm is not fatal, and that there is nothing to be feared from imperfectly pickled pigeons in the bug lab. "Doc" Minter is certain of a good practice if he wants it. He's got the right turn.

name: MOODY, CLAUDE EMMETT
clubs: Secretary Alpha Phi Epsilon Literary Society, '17
description: He is one of those fellows who gives us a time writing him up. He is so busy looking after his own affairs that he doesn't have time to get in the notice of the "common rabble." He is quiet, steady, and—yes—a lion among the ladies.

name: MORGAN, WALLACE WILLIAM
description: Wallace's chief asset is his cheerful friendliness. He is a strong believer in co-education, and specializes in campus courses. Having a great name, he aspires to great things. We are glad to say that he is willing to work for it, too. Watch him smile his way to success.

name: MORRIS, RUTH
description: It can be said without fear of contradiction that Ruth is a "weighty" girl. Her opinions and her discoveries are of great import. As a student, she has been perhaps the most thorough of any member of the class. Whether the subject in hand was Greek or Chemistry, she became its master. Her ambition is a worthy one. We know she will get there.

name: MOYE, LUTHER
description: He is a product of Florence Normal School, and is now taking a special course in poetry as a pre-requisite to the school of matrimony. See him smile and you will think that he has begun already. Luther is a good student, an excellent preacher, and a favorite with all the class—boys and girls.

name: NEWMAN, WALTER D.
description: One who doesn't worry until the time comes, and thinks the professor may forget and not call on him after all. He thinks wrong most of the time. He has added unto himself a wife since he entered College. She is his better half. He has a hand in everything that's done.

name: NIAGER, ROY
description: Personally he is inclined to be quiet. It is his purple bow tie that makes all the racket. He is a student who takes his work seriously. His specialties are Latin and Greek. With a few exceptions during the year they seem to agree with him quite well. He tried to correct the chapel attendance rule, but the faculty saw it differently.

name: PARSONS, ROBERT AUSTIN
clubs: President Alpha Phi Epsilon Literary Society
President of the Mystic Order of Bums
Representative of Alpha Phi Epsilon Literary Society in Inter-Society Debate
description: The presidency of nearly everything on the campus rarely means much, but "Pa" is the sort who fills any office he holds. The above flock of variegated responsibility ought to speak eloquently for his ability, but it is no measure of his popularity.

name: PITTARD, CLARENCE ROBERT
description: Clarence is a business man this year as well as a student. So he pays us little attention. It keeps him rather busy to keep up with his affairs at both "offices." All joking aside, he is an "all around goodfellow." He is buying two copies of the ENTRE-NOUS like some others are doing.

name: PRICE, CHARLES BENJAMIN, A. B.
description: Pooley likes to warble, he also likes to sleep; He likes to act the lover's part, and does it mighty neat. Now, what care he for lessons; why should he take a peep Into a musty "Bug" book, his pleasures to defeat?

name: PRICE, SIDNEY LANIER, A. B.
description: Cedric is like the proverbial little boy—always teasing girls, and he always lived up to his reputation at Howard. This is not all that Cedric can do, however, for he was instructor in Chemistry, an enthusiastic worker for the Crimson, and almost a misogynist. But he has changed his mind.

name: SENN, ANN LOUISE
description: Ann brings down all the honors on the tennis court from championship to tripping "the light fantastic." She captures her share of A's. And there is great speculation on the campus whether her future career will be on the farm or elsewhere.

name: SHELBURNE, KINGMAN CODY
description: Kingman's scholarship and work lost to 1920 one of its strongest members. He is neither a grind nor a shark, but he's what the faculty members mean when they say "a good student." He will be remembered for having a great affinity for chapel not at chapel time. Why? Well, everybody knows.

name: TRANT, JAMES B.
description: As he is president of the Y. M. C. A. and treasurer of most organizations to which he belongs, to comment on his morals would be superfluous. We are sure he would not like to have us dwell on either his sudden or brief love affairs, or his success in debating. J. B. is bound for an Eastern university to work for a higher degree. And then—

name: VAUGHN, JEWELL COMER
clubs: Representative of Alpha Phi Epsilon Literary Society in Inter-Society Debate
description: If you ever want a thing put across, just tell it to Jewell and, "mihi crede," it will get across in a jiffy. He is a student who puts his heart, mind and soul to each duty he has to perform. It is worth your while to take notice of him. Things are going to move for him.

name: WILLIAMS, CHARLES WESTON
description: A man who has stopped over with us from "Woco," Texas. He is bound for Oxford, England, with a Rhodes Scholarship in his valise. Woco is all business, and intends to make the world hear him. We are confident that he will succeed. We hope he will soon get back home and start the reformalution.
